items.q: How do you say 'I study English at school' in Spanish?
items.misconception: Estudio el inglés a la escuela.
items.why: Students add unnecessary articles or wrong prepositions. Languages as school subjects DROP the article: 'estudio inglés', 'me gusta el inglés' (general likes need article). Location: EN (not a) for places: en el colegio, en casa, en clase.

items.q: What is the gender of 'el día' (day)?
items.why: Students rely on '-a = feminine'. Common exceptions: el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el clima (many Greek-origin -ma words). These appear constantly so must be memorised.

items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' (in general) in Spanish?
items.why: English drops the article for general likes; Spanish keeps it. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: me gusta EL chocolate, me gustan LOS libros.

items.q: Which means 'English is more interesting than maths'?
items.correct: El inglés es más interesante que las matemáticas
items.misconception: El inglés es más que interesante matemáticas
items.random_wrong: El inglés es menos interesante las matemáticas
items.why: Correct answer: El inglés es más interesante que las matemáticas. “El inglés es más que interesante matemáticas” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: break time. “the timetable” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If classes start at 9 and finish at 3, how many hours is the school day?
items.why: The real figure is 6. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
